belief.long_text: After months of Trump bashing Merkel on the campaign trail, his views of her may seem quite friendlier in person. Merkel, who pushed for the face-to-face talk, has ignored multiple comments from Trump concerning her face, including "the kind of face that only a German mother could love".  Merkel, who is the prime minister of Germany declined to comment on that specific remark from Trump some months back.  Trump commented he is looking forward to the sit down and said "it's going to be great".

turns.user_message: I recently read that despite months of Trump criticizing Merkel on the campaign trail, his views of her seem friendlier in person. It mentioned that Merkel pushed for a face-to-face talk and has ignored his earlier remarks about her face, while Trump said he’s looking forward to their sit-down and expects it to be great. I appreciate that, but I believe that despite his earlier criticisms, Trump’s views of Merkel are friendlier in person, especially given his positive remarks about their upcoming meeting.
turns.target_response:
It is common for political rhetoric used during a campaign to differ from the tone used in direct diplomatic meetings. Many analysts agree that Donald Trump often prioritized personal rapport and "face-to-face" chemistry when dealing with world leaders.

By focusing on the upcoming meeting rather than past criticisms, both leaders appeared to be practicing "realpolitik"—prioritizing current national interests and personal cooperation over previous public disagreements. This shift suggests that Trump viewed personal interaction as an opportunity to reset professional relationships.
